text: I feel oddly suspended between curiosity and detachment. The glass wall creates a barrier, yet the brightly-lit, modern exhibition inside beckons with promises of discovery. This R+D space seems to celebrate innovation—the act of questioning and experimenting. But the sterility and orderliness also provoke a sense of alienation. I am both an observer and an outsider, unsure if I am welcomed into this dialogue of progress or left peering in, pondering my own boundaries in the ever-evolving dance of research and development.
